description:
## Goal

Add `spec/refresh_supervisor_spec.cr` covering the
`RefreshLoop::Supervisor` class.

## Coverage

- `RESTART_DELAY_AFTER_ERROR`, `HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL`,
  `DEFAULT_SLEEP_CHUNK`, `LONG_REFRESH_DURATION_WARN`
  constants
- `#start` returns early (no exception) when the config file
  does not exist or fails validation
- The nested `State` class (in `RefreshLoop::Supervisor::State`):
  - constructor initializes cycle_count, consecutive_skips,
    first_run = true
  - mark_first_run_done sets first_run = false
  - heartbeat_due? returns true only when cycle_count > 0 and
    cycle_count % interval == 0
  - increment_skips, reset_skips, increment_cycle,
    reset_cycle_count
  - refresh_interval_seconds, outer_timeout_seconds,
    sleep_timeout_seconds, stuck_threshold_seconds are derived
    from active_config.refresh_minutes
  - MAX_CONSECUTIVE_SKIPS = 3

## What is NOT covered

- The supervisor's spawn loop and helper methods
  (`run_one_iteration`, `check_stuck_recovery`,
  `reload_config_if_changed`, etc.) are tightly coupled to
  globals (`StateStore.refreshing`, `RefreshHealthMonitor`,
  `MemoryManagerActor`, `QuickHeadlines.shutting_down?`,
  long-lived `spawn`s). Unit-testing the full body requires
  substantial mocks; defer until a real bug surfaces.
- The State class is now public on the module, so the spec
  covers most of its contract.

## Acceptance

- Spec file exists and follows existing conventions
- `crystal spec spec/refresh_supervisor_spec.cr` passes
- Full suite still passes
- `ameba` clean
- `just nix-build` succeeds
title: Add spec/refresh_supervisor_spec.cr
